folio.label: 57r
folio.l: ¢ The day is comen , of here departynge
I say the wooful day , fatal , is come
That there may be , no lengere tariynge
And forthward they hem dressen , alle & some
Custaunce that was , with sorwe al ouäcome
Ful pale a rist , and dressitú hire to wende
For wel she setú , æäe is noon other ende
¢ Allas what wonder is it , thougú she wepte
That shal be sent , to straunge nacione
Fro frendes , æat so tenderly hire kepte
And to be bounde , vnder subieccione
Of oon , she knoweæ nou©t , his condicione
Housbondes ben alle goode , and han ben ©ore
That knowen wyues , I dar sey ©ow nomore
¢ Fader she seide , thy wrecched child Custaunce
Thy ©onge dougúter , fostred vp so softe
And ©e my moder , my souereigne plesaunce
Ouer alle thynge , out take crist on loft´
Custaunce ©oure child , hire recomaundetú oft´
Vn to ©oure grace , for I shal to Surrye
Ne shal I neuäe , sen ©ow more witú eye
¢ Allas vn to the barbre , nacione
I must goon , syn that it is ©oure wylle
But crist that starf , for oure sauacione
So ©eue me grace , hise hestes to fulfille
I wrecched wo~man , no fors thougú I spille
Wo~men arn born~ , to thraldom & penaunce
And to ben , vnder ma~nes gouänance

note.notetxt: 189Thre is a cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: ¢ I trowe at Troye , whan Pirrus brak the wal
Or Ylyon brende , Thebes the Citee
Ne at Rome , for the harme thurgú hanybal
That Romeyns han venquyssed , tymes three
Nas herd , swicú tendre wepynge , for pitee
As in the chaumbre was , for hire departynge
But fortú she moot , wheiæä so she wepe or synge
¢ O first meeuyngö , cruel firmament
Witú thy dyurnal sweigú , that cronwedestcrowdestcronwedest aycrouwedest198the underdotting is done with a darker ink: the same that inserted the gloss.ODR
And hurlest alle , fro Est til Occident
That naturelly wolde holde , a nother wey
Thy crowdyngö sette the heuene , in swicú array
At the bygynnynge , of this fiers viage
That cruel Mars , hatú slayn this mariage
¢ Infortunat ascendent tortuous
Of whicú the lord , is helplees falle allas
Ou©t of his angle , in to the derkest hous
